meta.title: Microsoft Entra ID – Plans, Features & AD DS Comparison
meta.subject: Microsoft Identity & Access Management

questions.question_text: Which feature is unique to the Microsoft Entra ID P2 plan and not included in the P1 plan or the free version?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Self-service group management is available in the P1 plan, not exclusive to P2.

b.explanation: Correct. Microsoft Entra Identity Protection is a P2-exclusive feature. It provides risk-based conditional access and automated responses to detected identity risks, which are not available in P1 or the Free tier.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Cloud App Discovery (part of Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps) is available at the P1 level and is not exclusive to P2.

questions.question_text: What is a significant difference between how organizational structures are managed in Microsoft Entra ID compared to AD DS?
questions.topic: Entra ID vs AD DS – Structure

a.text: Microsoft Entra ID and AD DS both use Group Policies to manage organizational structures.
a.explanation: Incorrect. Group Policy Objects (GPOs) are an AD DS concept. Microsoft Entra ID does not use GPOs for managing organizational structures.

b.text: Microsoft Entra ID uses a flat structure without OUs, while AD DS uses a hierarchical structure with OUs.
b.explanation: Correct. AD DS organizes objects into a hierarchical tree using Organizational Units (OUs), which enables granular Group Policy application. Microsoft Entra ID uses a flat directory model without OUs, relying on groups and administrative units instead.

c.text: Both Microsoft Entra ID and AD DS use hierarchical structures with Organizational Units (OUs).
c.explanation: Incorrect. Only AD DS uses OUs in a hierarchy. Microsoft Entra ID has a flat structure and does not support OUs in the traditional AD DS sense.

questions.question_text: Which Microsoft Entra ID feature, available in P2 but not in P1, allows for enhanced monitoring and protection of user accounts?

a.explanation: Correct. Microsoft Entra Identity Protection is a P2-exclusive capability. It monitors sign-in risk and user risk signals, generates risk reports, and can enforce risk-based Conditional Access policies to automatically protect accounts.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Microsoft Entra Connect Health monitors the health of your on-premises identity infrastructure and is included with Microsoft Entra ID P1, not exclusive to P2.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Microsoft Identity Manager (MIM) is a separate on-premises identity governance product and is not a feature tier within Microsoft Entra ID plans.

questions.question_text: Which feature distinguishes Microsoft Entra ID from AD DS in terms of application management?
questions.topic: Entra ID vs AD DS – Application Management

a.text: Service principal objects for cross-tenant application usage.
a.explanation: Correct. Microsoft Entra ID uses service principal objects and application registrations to manage access for cloud and multi-tenant applications — a capability that does not exist in traditional AD DS.

b.text: Use of Group Policy Objects (GPOs) for application deployment.
b.explanation: Incorrect. GPOs are an AD DS feature used for on-premises application and policy deployment. Microsoft Entra ID does not use GPOs.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Kerberos is used by AD DS for on-premises authentication. Microsoft Entra ID primarily uses modern protocols like OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect for application authentication.

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes the primary focus of Microsoft Entra ID compared to AD DS?

a.text: Creating organizational units for policy application.
a.explanation: Incorrect. Creating OUs for policy application is the domain of AD DS, not Microsoft Entra ID, which has a flat directory structure.

b.text: Providing identity management services to web-based applications.
b.explanation: Correct. Microsoft Entra ID is designed primarily as a cloud-based identity provider for web and SaaS applications, supporting modern authentication protocols like OAuth 2.0 and SAML. This contrasts with AD DS, which focuses on managing on-premises network resources.

c.text: Managing computer objects within a corporate network.
c.explanation: Incorrect. Managing computer objects and network resources is the primary focus of AD DS. Microsoft Entra ID is oriented toward cloud and web-based identity management.

questions.question_text: An organization using AD DS is planning to extend its on-premises infrastructure to Microsoft Entra ID. What feature of Microsoft Entra ID can help manage user authentication across both environments?

a.explanation: Incorrect. GPOs are an on-premises AD DS feature and do not extend to or operate within Microsoft Entra ID for cross-environment authentication.

b.explanation: Correct. Federation (using technologies like AD FS or Microsoft Entra ID federation) allows organizations to establish a trust relationship between AD DS and Microsoft Entra ID, enabling seamless single sign-on across both on-premises and cloud environments. Microsoft Entra Connect can also synchronize identities to support hybrid scenarios.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Kerberos is an on-premises AD DS authentication protocol and is not natively used by Microsoft Entra ID for cloud authentication. It cannot bridge the two environments on its own.

questions.question_text: Your organization requires advanced security reports and alerts for cloud applications. Which Microsoft Entra ID plan should you choose to meet this requirement?

a.explanation: Incorrect. While P1 includes several security features, advanced security reports, risk detections, and automated alerts for cloud applications are part of the P2 tier through features like Identity Protection.

b.explanation: Incorrect. The Free tier provides only basic security features and does not include advanced security reporting or cloud app risk alerts.

c.explanation: Correct. Microsoft Entra ID P2 includes advanced security capabilities such as Identity Protection with risk-based reports, alerts, and automated remediation policies for cloud applications — beyond what P1 or Free offer.

questions.question_text: Your organization wants to ensure that only users from its Microsoft Entra tenant can access a specific Azure web application. Which configuration should be applied in the Azure portal to achieve this?

a.text: Use LDAP queries to restrict access
a.explanation: Incorrect. LDAP is an on-premises AD DS protocol and is not used by Azure App Service for controlling tenant-based access to web applications.

b.text: Implement Kerberos authentication for the web application
b.explanation: Incorrect. Kerberos is an on-premises authentication protocol. Azure web applications use modern protocols like OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, not Kerberos.

c.text: Designate the Microsoft Entra tenant in the Authentication/Authorization blade
c.explanation: Correct. In the Azure portal, navigating to the App Service's Authentication/Authorization blade and configuring Microsoft Entra ID as the identity provider — specifying the organization's tenant — ensures only users from that tenant can authenticate and access the application.

questions.question_text: A developer needs to ensure that an Azure App Service web application only allows access to users from a specific Microsoft Entra tenant. How can this be achieved in the Azure portal?

a.explanation: Incorrect. DNS domain name restrictions are not an authentication mechanism in Azure App Service and would not enforce tenant-based identity controls.

b.text: Configure Microsoft Entra authentication in the Authentication/Authorization blade
b.explanation: Correct. The Authentication/Authorization blade in Azure App Service allows developers to enable Microsoft Entra ID as the identity provider and restrict access to users within a specific tenant. This is the standard and recommended approach.

c.text: Set up Kerberos authentication for the app
c.explanation: Incorrect. Kerberos is an on-premises Windows authentication protocol and is not applicable for tenant-level access restrictions on Azure App Service hosted in the cloud.
